author: śrīla sārvabhauma bhaṭṭācārya
verses:
ujjvala⋅varaṇa⋅gaura⋅vara⋅dehaṁ
vilasita⋅niravadhi⋅bhāva⋅videham
tribhuvana⋅pāvana⋅kṛpāyā leśaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

gadgada⋅antara⋅bhāva⋅vikāraṁ
durjana⋅tarjana⋅nāda⋅viśālam
bhava⋅bhaya⋅bhañjana⋅kāraṇa⋅karuṇaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

aruṇāmvara⋅dhara⋅cārukapolaṁ
indu⋅vinindita⋅nakha⋅caya⋅ruciram
jalpita⋅nija⋅guṇa⋅nāma⋅vinodaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

vigalita⋅nayana⋅kamala⋅jala⋅dhāraṁ
bhūṣaṇa⋅nava⋅rasa⋅bhāva⋅vikāram
gati⋅atimanthara⋅nṛtya⋅vilāsaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

cañcala⋅cāru⋅caraṇa⋅gati⋅ruciraṁ
mañjīra⋅rañjita⋅pada⋅yuga⋅madhuram
candra⋅vinindita⋅śītala⋅vadanaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

dhṛta⋅kaṭi⋅ḍora⋅kamaṇḍalu⋅daṇḍaṁ
divya⋅kalevara⋅muṇḍita⋅muṇḍam
durjana⋅kalmaṣa⋅khaṇḍana⋅daṇḍaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

bhūṣaṇa⋅bhū⋅raja⋅alakā⋅valitaṁ
kampita⋅vimvādhara⋅vara⋅ruciram
malayaja⋅viracita⋅ujjvala⋅tilakaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam

nindita⋅aruṇa⋅kamala⋅dala⋅nayanaṁ
ājānulambita⋅śrī⋅bhuja⋅yugalam
kalevara⋅kaiśora⋅nartaka⋅veśaṁ
taṁ praṇamāmi ca śrī⋅śacī⋅tanayam
en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose body is of a bright, golden complexion and who is ever filled with ecstatic love. He possesses a trace of compassion that purifies the three worlds.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose heart is overwhelmed with emotions, whose loud roars threaten the wicked, and who is the merciful cause of destroying the fear of material existence.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who wears saffron garments, has beautiful cheeks, and whose toenails are more radiant than the moon. He delights in chanting His own names and qualities.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, from whose lotus-like eyes a stream of tears flows, whose body exhibits new and fresh ecstatic emotions, and who dances with extremely slow, graceful movements.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose charming and restless foot movements are beautiful, whose sweet feet are adorned with ankle bells, and whose cool face surpasses the moon.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, who wears a waist-string, carries a water pot and a staff, whose divine body has a shaven head, and whose staff dispels the sins of the wicked.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose body is adorned with dust and curly hair, whose trembling red lips are beautiful, and who is marked with a bright tilaka made of sandalwood paste.

I offer my obeisances to the son of Śacī, whose eyes shame the red lotus petals, whose divine arms extend down to His knees, and whose youthful body is dressed as a dancer.
